The blue dots are at [16,48], [24,40], [24,53], [35,48].

2. Player can move the yellow cross around the board
3. The goal might be to position the yellow cross to cover/collect the yellow dots or blue dots
Let me check if the yellow cross arms can collect the yellow dots. The yellow dots are at [3,15], [9,6], [9,24], [17,15].
The yellow cross arms are 11px long each, extending from the center. So the arms cover:
- Top arm: rows [center_row-10, center_row-1] at center_col
- Bottom arm: rows [center_row+1, center_row+10] at center_col
- Left arm: cols [center_col-10, center_col-1] at center_row
- Right arm: cols [center_col+1, center_col+10] at center_row
To collect a yellow dot at [r, c], the cross center needs to be at:
- [r+10, c] (dot on top arm) or [r-10, c] (dot on bottom arm) or [r, c+10] (dot on left arm) or [r, c-10] (dot on right arm)
Let me check the yellow dots:
- [3,15]: center at [13,15] (top arm), [-7,15] (bottom, invalid), [3,25] (left arm), [3,5] (right arm)
- [9,6]: center at [19,6], [-1,6] (invalid), [9,16], [9,-4] (invalid)
- [9,24]: center at [19,24], [-1,24] (invalid), [9,34], [9,14]
- [17,15]: center at [27,15], [7,15], [17,25], [17,5]
So to collect all 4 yellow dots, the cross center needs to be at positions that cover all 4 dots. But the cross can only be at one position at a time. So maybe the goal is to collect dots one at a time by moving the cross over them.
Let me try moving the cross to cover one of the yellow dots and see if it gets collected.
The yellow dot at [17,15] can be covered by centering the cross at [27,15] (dot on top arm) or [7,15] (dot on bottom arm) or [17,25] (dot on left arm) or [17,5] (dot on right arm).
Currently the player is at [30,24]. Let me move the cross to [27,15] to cover the yellow dot at [17,15].
Actually, wait. Let me first check if the yellow cross arms actually collect dots when they overlap. Let me move the cross to a position where one arm overlaps a yellow dot.
The yellow dot at [17,15] is at row 17, col 15. If the cross center is at [27,15], the top arm covers rows 17-26 at col 15. So the dot at [17,15] would be on the top arm.

sections.label: TOOL CALL: python
sections.content:
<tool_call>
<function=python>
<parameter=code>
# Move cross to [27,15] to cover yellow dot at [17,15] with top arm
# Current: [30,24], target: [27,15]
# Need: UP 3, LEFT 9
moves = ['UP']*3 + ['LEFT']*9
action(moves)

nodes = current_frame.segmentation['nodes']
from collections import defaultdict
by_color = defaultdict(list)
for n in nodes:
    by_color[n['color']].append(n)

print("After moving to [27,15]:")
for n in by_color['W']:
    print(f"  White: boundary={n['boundary']}")
for n in by_color['Y']:
    b = n['boundary']
    if b:
        rows = [p[0] for p in b]
        cols = [p[1] for p in b]
        if n['pixels'] > 5:
            print(f"  Yellow: bounds=[{min(rows)},{min(cols)}]-[{max(rows)},{max(cols)}] pixels={n['pixels']}")
        else:
            print(f"  Yellow dot: boundary={b}")
print(f"  Yellow dots count: {sum(1 for n in by_color['Y'] if n['pixels'] <= 1)}")

print(f"\nResult: {last_action_result}")
</parameter>
</function>
</tool_call>
